query:
Your agent ID: 437-investigator

Bug report (GH Issue #437):

Title: bug: recurring_friction and dead_knowledge extractors write KM signals to ENTRIES instead of maintenance_recommendations

## Problem

Two extractors in unimatrix-observe produce ProposedEntry objects with created_by: "background-tick" that are written into the ENTRIES table as lesson-learned entries:

- crates/unimatrix-observe/src/extraction/recurring_friction.rs → "Recurring friction: {rule}" entries
- crates/unimatrix-observe/src/extraction/dead_knowledge.rs → "Possible dead knowledge: {title}" entries

These are knowledge management signals, not knowledge. They describe observations about the state of the knowledge base itself ("rule X fired in N sessions", "entry Y was not accessed recently"). Storing them as ENTRIES means they:

- Pollute semantic search with low-value, repetitive content
- Consume HNSW vector slots
- Accumulate indefinitely as Active entries
- Confidence ≈ 0.29 — the lowest in the corpus

## Wrong output path

The maintenance_recommendations field in context_status already exists for surfacing exactly this kind of observation. Both signals belong there, not in ENTRIES.

## Fix

- Route recurring_friction findings to maintenance_recommendations (not ProposedEntry)
- Route dead_knowledge findings to maintenance_recommendations (not ProposedEntry)
- Remove the ENTRIES write path from both extractors

## Cleanup

Existing entries in ENTRIES with created_by = "background-tick" in lesson-learned category need to be quarantined. These are identifiable by title prefix ("Recurring friction:", "Possible dead knowledge:") and creator field.

## Related

- #430 — same class of bug: wrong write path for operational data (session outcomes → ENTRIES instead of OUTCOME_INDEX)

Affected area: crates/unimatrix-observe/src/extraction/ (recurring_friction.rs, dead_knowledge.rs), maintenance_recommendations wiring, context_status handler, background tick logic

Prior knowledge from Unimatrix search:
- Entry #3252: ExtractionRule must not perform side effects: maintenance actions belong in maintenance_tick, not in ProposedEntry output (lesson-learned, bugfix-351)
- Entry #3254: ExtractionRule vs maintenance action: additive rules must not signal deprecation (pattern, GH #351)
- Entry #3312: dead_knowledge observation snippet must use id: N or #N format (pattern)

Your tasks:
1. Investigate both extractor files — read their full implementation to understand exactly what they produce
2. Trace the call chain: how does ProposedEntry output from these extractors get written to ENTRIES?
3. Identify where maintenance_recommendations is populated and how to route the signals there instead
4. Understand the cleanup path for existing background-tick entries in ENTRIES
5. Identify the minimal set of files to change
6. Assess blast radius: what else calls these extractors, what tests exist

Return:
- Root cause analysis
- Affected files and functions (paths)
- Proposed fix approach (specific, actionable, step by step)
- Risk assessment
- Missing test identification
- Write your agent report to /workspaces/unimatrix/product/features/bugfix-437/agents/437-investigator-report.md
- Post diagnosis as a comment on GH Issue #437

entries.title: ExtractionRule vs maintenance action: additive rules must not signal deprecation (GH #351)
dead_knowledge observation snippet must use \"id\": N or #N format — other formats silently ignored
ADR-006: Convert ExtractionRule::evaluate() to async fn
Renaming a detection rule requires updating 5+ string-keyed match arms across two crates plus all agent-facing SKILL.md files
Agent reports omit Knowledge Stewardship section unless structurally enforced — recurring pattern (col-022, col-028, nan-009, crt-030)
